associatedMedia.headline: Cowthally Castle, Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Scotland
associatedMedia.description:
Cowthally Castle, view of a ruined overgrown castle with remnants of a round tower to the left, on a hill-top with a stream running into the foreground to right, a spire among trees in the distance to right and mountains in the background; illustration to Scott's edition of Somerville's 'Memorie of the Somervilles ...'. 1815
Print made by: Richard Rhodes

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Janet Stewart是Alexander Stewart和Janet Turnbull的女儿，于大约1325年出生。她于大约1348年与Thomas de Sommerville结婚。她去世于1407年在苏格兰藍納可爾卡瓦斯享年83岁，埋葬在苏格兰藍納可爾卡瓦斯。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Janet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a diminutive of   Jane  , already in common use in the Middle English period. It remained in use in Scotland and in some parts of England well into the 17th century and was revived at the end of the 19th century to much more widespread use, while still retaining its popularity in Scotland. Since the 1960s, however, it has rather gone out of fashion in Britain.

variants.name: Jannet
meanings: Originally a diminutive of   Jane  , already in common use in the Middle English period. It remained in use in Scotland and in some parts of England well into the 17th century and was revived at the end of the 19th century to much more widespread use, while still retaining its popularity in Scotland. Since the 1960s, however, it has rather gone out of fashion in Britain.

en.lifeSummary: Janet Stewart was born about 1325 as the daughter of Alexander Stewart and Janet Turnbull. She married Thomas de Sommerville about 1348. She died in 1407, in Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Scotland, at the age of 83, and was buried in Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Janet Stewart est née environ 1325, fille de Alexander Stewart et Janet Turnbull. Elle a épousé Thomas de Sommerville environ 1348. Elle est décédée en 1407, à Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterrée à Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Janet Stewart wurde um 1325, als Tochter von Alexander Stewart und Janet Turnbull geboren. Sie hat um 1348 Thomas de Sommerville geheiratet. Sie starb 1407 im Alter von 83 Jahren in Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Scotland und wurde in Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Janet Stewart è nata il circa 1325 come figlia di Alexander Stewart e Janet Turnbull. Lei sposato Thomas de Sommerville circa 1348. Morì nel 1407, nel Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Scotland, all'età di 83 anni, e fu sepolta a Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Janet Stewartは約1325年にAlexander StewartとJanet Turnbullの娘として生まれた。約1348年にThomas de Sommervilleと結婚した。彼女は1407年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアカーンワスで83歳で死去し，スコットランドラナルクシュアイアカーンワスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Janet Stewart(은)는 대략 1325년 Alexander Stewart(와)과 Janet Turnbull의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1348년 Thomas de Sommerville(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1407년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 카른왓h에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 카른왓h에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Janet Stewart nasceu aproximadamente 1325 filha de Alexander Stewart e Janet Turnbull. Ela casou-se com Thomas de Sommerville aproximadamente 1348. Ela faleceu em 1407, em Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Scotland, com 83 anos, e foi sepultada em Carnwath, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Janet Stewart родилась около 1325 г. Её родителей звали Alexander Stewart и Janet Turnbull. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas de Sommerville около 1348 г. Она умерла в 1407 г. в местности карнват, ланаркшир, Шотландия в возрасте 83 лет и была похоронена в местности карнват, ланаркшир, Шотландия.
